title: Microcos mortehanii
intro: Microcos mortehanii, also known as the Mortehani's microcos, is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Microcos mortehanii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit potential therapeutic benefits. In traditional herbal medicine, Microcos mortehanii is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being made into capsules or tablets, to take advantage of its health-promoting properties.
